S&P Dow Jones Indices will make the following changes to the S&P MidCap 400 index effective prior to the open of trading on Monday, June 22, 2026, to coincide with the quarterly rebalance - the S&P MidCap 400 index is adding Roku (ROKU), Coeur Mining (CDE), Semtech (SMTC), Sanmina (SANM), and Viavi Solutions (VIAV), and is deleting Flex (FLEX), BellRing Brands (BRBR), Coty (COTY), Concentrix (CNXC), and Blackbaud (BLKB). The changes ensure that the index is more representative of its market capitalization range. The companies being removed from S&P MidCap 400 are no longer representative of the mid-cap market space.

This watchlist reflects a classic “communications + memory + semiconductor recovery” expansion theme, typically seen in early-cycle rotation setups. 
 
NOK is transitioning from legacy telecom equipment toward 5G and network infrastructure exposure. VIAV benefits from a rebound in optical communications and network testing demand. SNDK (SanDisk) is leveraged to the memory cycle recovery and AI-driven NAND demand growth. AVEX is more of a small-cap, high-volatility trading name. 
 
The core narrative is that AI is not only driving GPUs, but also lifting demand across communications networks, memory, and testing equipment. 
 
However, these setups typically share common traits: cyclical sensitivity, sentiment-driven moves, and lagging fundamental validation. Short-term upside can be strong, but sustainability depends on whether the semiconductor upcycle is truly broadening. 
 
In this semiconductor expansion phase, do you prefer cyclical core exposure like SNDK, or infrastructure recovery plays like VIAV and NOK?

Fitch Ratings upgraded Viavi Solutions to BB from BB- with a stable outlook after the company repaid $450 million in term loan debt using proceeds from a recent equity offering. Fitch also raised the rating on Viavi’s senior unsecured notes to BB from BB-. 
The upgrade reflects a significant improvement in leverage, with EBITDA leverage for fiscal 2026 expected to decline to 2.6x from 5.1x. Viavi used net proceeds of about $557 million from the stock issuance to fully repay its first-lien secured term loan balance, strengthening its balance sheet and financial flexibility. 
Viavi operates in wireless and wired network testing and measurement markets, with most revenue generated from its Network Enablement and Services segment, while the remainder comes from Optical Security and Performance Products. The company also expanded through the 2025 acquisitions of Inertial Labs and certain high-speed ethernet testing assets from Spirent Communications.
